title: Lecture: Origins of Jazz

segments.text: Jazz emerged in the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries in New Orleans.
It grew out of a blend of African rhythms, blues, ragtime, and European harmonic structures.
What made jazz distinct was its emphasis on improvisation — musicians would take a melody and reinterpret it in real time.
By the nineteen twenties, jazz had spread north to Chicago and New York, and then around the world.

transcript: Jazz emerged in the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries in New Orleans. It grew out of a blend of African rhythms, blues, ragtime, and European harmonic structures. What made jazz distinct was its emphasis on improvisation — musicians would take a melody and reinterpret it in real time. By the nineteen twenties, jazz had spread north to Chicago and New York, and then around the world. It transformed popular music forever.
